prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Benzene HOMO, LUMO energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier "standard"; density fitting default on) in PySCF  (backend reported as "pyscf")
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz).  Single-point calculation; no geometry re-optimization at DFT (notation: DFT//xyz-file).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles.  No warnings were reported by the engine (`warnings` array empty).  Integrity check status: ok (trustworthy = true).  Calculation used density-fitted integrals (RIJK) as per chemkit’s PySCF default.

Results (key orbital energies)
  HOMO energy  : −7.0468329 eV
  LUMO energy  : −0.41574728 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-type): 6.6310857 eV

Live log & files: The run streamed its detailed output to the tool-generated .out log in the working directory (path provided at launch) and wrote the structured results to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpuicnmccd/live.json.

All data were obtained by the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill with the options:
  skill = frontier-orbitals, method = dft (functional B3LYP, basis def2-tzvp, tier standard), charge 0, multiplicity 1, gas phase.

The headline numerical result is the HOMO–LUMO gap:
  6.6310857 eV
